text: What is electronegativity?
choices: The ability of an atom to attract shared electrons in a covalent bond
The total number of neutrons in an atom’s nucleus
The ability of a molecule to dissolve in water
The force that holds opposite ions together in a crystal
explain: Electronegativity describes how strongly an atom pulls shared electrons toward itself in a bond.

text: What is a polar covalent bond?
choices: A bond in which electrons are shared equally
A bond in which electrons are shared unequally because one atom attracts them more strongly
A bond formed by complete transfer of electrons
A weak attraction between two water molecules
explain: Unequal sharing of electrons creates partial charges and a polar covalent bond.

text: What is an ionic bond?
choices: A bond formed when atoms share electrons unequally
A bond formed by the attraction between oppositely charged ions
A bond that links amino acids together
A bond found only in DNA
explain: Ionic bonds arise from electron transfer and electrostatic attraction between ions.

text: What is a structural isomer?
choices: A molecule with a different number of atoms but the same shape
A molecule with the same molecular formula as another but a different covalent arrangement of atoms
A molecule that changes shape only when heated
A protein with more than one subunit
explain: Structural isomers share a molecular formula but differ in how atoms are connected.

text: What is the main function of starch in plants?
choices: Structural support in cell walls
Long-term information storage
Energy storage
Catalyzing reactions
explain: Starch is a plant storage polysaccharide used to store energy as glucose units.

text: What is the main function of glycogen in animals?
choices: Structural support outside the cell
Energy storage
Membrane formation
Base pairing in DNA
explain: Glycogen is the short-term storage form of glucose in animals.

text: Which carbohydrate is most associated with structural support in plants?
choices: Glycogen
Cellulose
Starch
Sucrose
explain: Cellulose provides structural support in plant cell walls due to β-linkages between glucose units.

text: What is the role of the phosphate group in a nucleotide?
choices: It helps form part of the sugar-phosphate backbone of nucleic acids
It determines the amino acid sequence of a protein
It acts as the variable R group of the nucleotide
It replaces the nitrogenous base in RNA
explain: Phosphate groups link sugars together to form the backbone of DNA and RNA.

text: What is the nitrogenous base-pairing rule in DNA?
choices: A pairs with C, and G pairs with T
A pairs with U, and C pairs with G
A pairs with T, and C pairs with G
A pairs with G, and T pairs with C
explain: In DNA, adenine pairs with thymine and cytosine pairs with guanine.

text: Why are buffers important in living organisms?
choices: They prevent all chemical reactions from happening too fast
They stabilize pH, helping molecules keep proper structure and function
They dissolve lipids permanently in water
They convert acids directly into sugars
explain: Buffers resist changes in pH, protecting the structure and function of biomolecules like proteins.

text: A molecule contains oxygen and hydrogen atoms joined by polar covalent bonds. Because of its bent shape, one side of the molecule is slightly negative and the other side is slightly positive. What property is most likely to result?
choices: The molecule will be nonpolar and unable to interact with other polar molecules
The molecule can form hydrogen bonds with other polar molecules
The molecule must be a lipid
The molecule can form only ionic bonds and no covalent bonds
explain: Polar geometry and partial charges enable hydrogen bonding with other polar molecules.

text: A carbohydrate polymer is synthesized in a cell by repeatedly adding glucose monomers to a growing chain. Which event must occur during each addition?
choices: A water molecule is removed as a covalent bond forms
A peptide bond is broken by hydrolysis
A phosphate group is inserted between the monomers
An amino acid is attached to the chain
explain: Building polymers from monomers typically involves dehydration synthesis, removing water as bonds form.

text: A scientist compares two fatty acid samples. Sample 1 has only single bonds between carbons. Sample 2 contains several double bonds. At the same temperature, Sample 1 is more solid. Which explanation is best?
choices: Double bonds in Sample 2 create bends that reduce close packing
Single bonds in Sample 1 make the molecules polar
Sample 2 contains more peptide bonds, which lowers melting point
Sample 1 must contain more nucleotides
explain: Double bonds introduce kinks that reduce tight packing and lower melting point; saturated fats pack more tightly.

text: A mutation changes a nucleotide in DNA, and the protein produced now contains an amino acid with a side chain of very different polarity. Why could this affect function?
choices: Protein function often depends on how amino acid side chains interact during folding and binding
A change in one amino acid always makes the protein longer
DNA mutations cannot affect proteins
The protein will necessarily lose all covalent bonds
explain: Side-chain properties influence folding and interactions; changes can alter structure and function.

text: A researcher notes that a molecule dissolves well in water but not in oil. Which conclusion is most reasonable?
choices: The molecule is likely mostly nonpolar
The molecule is likely polar or charged
The molecule must be a triglyceride
The molecule cannot participate in any intermolecular interactions
explain: Polar or charged molecules dissolve better in water than in nonpolar solvents like oil.

text: Two proteins have the same number of amino acids, but one functions as an enzyme and the other as a structural fiber. Which statement best explains this difference?
choices: Proteins with the same length must have the same function
Differences in amino acid sequence and resulting shape can produce different functions
One of the molecules must actually be a carbohydrate
Functional differences arise only from the number of peptide bonds
explain: Sequence and folding determine structure and function, not just length.

text: A teacher tells students that pH is logarithmic. Which conclusion follows from this?
choices: A solution with pH 4 has slightly more H+ than a solution with pH 5
A solution with pH 4 has ten times the hydrogen ion concentration of a solution with pH 5
A solution with pH 4 is neutral
A solution with pH 5 is always basic
explain: Each pH unit represents a tenfold change in hydrogen ion concentration.

text: A phospholipid and a triglyceride both contain glycerol and fatty acids. Why do they often serve different biological roles?
choices: Phospholipids are amphipathic, while triglycerides are mostly nonpolar
Triglycerides are nucleic acids, while phospholipids are proteins
Phospholipids are made of amino acids
Triglycerides contain no carbon
explain: Phospholipids have polar headgroups that enable membrane formation; triglycerides are mainly hydrophobic energy stores.

text: A student says, “Cellulose and glycogen both contain glucose, so human enzymes should digest them equally well.” Which response is best?
choices: The student is correct because all glucose polymers have identical bonds
The student is incorrect because bond orientation and structure affect whether enzymes can break the polymer
The student is incorrect because glycogen is made of amino acids
The student is correct because all carbohydrates are structurally identical
explain: Enzyme recognition depends on linkage type and polymer structure; orientation matters for digestibility.

text: A protein that normally works in the cytoplasm is experimentally moved into a highly nonpolar environment. Which amino acid side chains would become less favorable on its outer surface?
choices: Nonpolar side chains
Polar and charged side chains
Only glycine
Only cysteine
explain: Polar and charged residues are stabilized by aqueous environments and are less favorable when exposed to nonpolar surroundings.

text:
Students measure pH before and after adding the same amount of acid to two solutions.

Solution A:
Initial pH = 7.0
Final pH = 6.9

Solution B:
Initial pH = 7.0
Final pH = 4.8

Which interpretation is best supported?
choices: Solution A likely has greater buffering capacity than Solution B
Solution B is the better buffer because its pH changed more
Solution A contains no hydrogen ions
Solution B must contain more proteins
explain: A smaller pH change after adding acid indicates stronger buffering capacity.
experiment: Equal amounts of acid were added to two solutions and pH changes were measured; the smaller change indicates better buffering.

text: A group of students tests an unknown sample with iodine solution. The sample turns blue-black. Which conclusion is most reasonable?
choices: The sample likely contains starch
The sample likely contains many peptide bonds
The sample must be a lipid because it changed color
The sample must contain DNA because it is dark
explain: Iodine forms a blue-black complex with starch (amylose), indicating its presence.
experiment: An unknown sample was exposed to iodine and observed for a color change consistent with starch.

text: Researchers compare two versions of the same protein. In version X, a nonpolar amino acid in the interior is replaced with a charged amino acid. Version X folds poorly and loses function. Which explanation best fits the result?
choices: The mutation likely disrupted hydrophobic interactions that normally stabilize the protein interior
The mutation removed all peptide bonds from the protein
The mutation changed the protein into a phospholipid
The mutation proves the primary structure is unrelated to tertiary structure
explain: Introducing a charged residue into a hydrophobic core disrupts packing and destabilizes folding.

text: A scientist heats equal volumes of water and a nonpolar oil for the same amount of time. Water shows a smaller temperature increase. Which conclusion is best supported?
choices: Water has a higher specific heat, likely due to hydrogen bonding among water molecules
Oil contains no atoms, so it heats faster
Water cannot absorb energy
Oil must be more acidic than water
explain: Water's hydrogen bonding gives it a higher specific heat, so it warms less under equal heating.

text: A mutation changes the DNA sequence of a gene, but the resulting protein shows no change in amino acid sequence. Which explanation is most likely?
choices: The mutation was silent because more than one codon can specify the same amino acid
DNA mutations can never affect proteins
The protein must have been destroyed before measurement
The mutation converted the gene into RNA
explain: Redundancy of the genetic code allows some nucleotide changes to leave amino acid sequence unchanged (silent mutation).

text:
Students analyze three biological samples.

Sample 1:
Positive Benedict’s test
Negative Biuret test
Negative iodine test

Sample 2:
Negative Benedict’s test
Negative Biuret test
Positive iodine test

Sample 3:
Negative Benedict’s test
Positive Biuret test
Negative iodine test

Which matching is most likely?
choices: Sample 1 = reducing sugar, Sample 2 = starch, Sample 3 = protein
Sample 1 = protein, Sample 2 = lipid, Sample 3 = starch
Sample 1 = starch, Sample 2 = reducing sugar, Sample 3 = nucleic acid
Sample 1 = lipid, Sample 2 = protein, Sample 3 = reducing sugar
explain: Benedict positive suggests reducing sugar, iodine positive suggests starch, and Biuret positive indicates protein.

text: A student compares two nucleic acid molecules. Molecule 1 contains deoxyribose and is usually double-stranded. Molecule 2 contains ribose and is usually single-stranded. Which conclusion is best supported?
choices: Molecule 1 is DNA and Molecule 2 is RNA
Molecule 1 is a protein and Molecule 2 is a lipid
Molecule 1 and Molecule 2 are both carbohydrates
Molecule 2 must contain thymine because it is single-stranded
explain: Deoxyribose and double-stranded structure identify DNA; ribose and single-stranded structure identify RNA.

text: Researchers create an artificial membrane from phospholipids and place it in water. The membrane self-assembles with hydrophilic regions facing outward and hydrophobic regions facing inward. Which principle does this best demonstrate?
choices: Structure and polarity of molecules influence how they organize and function
All lipids dissolve completely in water
Fatty acids always form covalent bonds with water
Biological molecules organize randomly with no predictable pattern
explain: Amphipathic structure and polarity drive predictable self-assembly such as bilayer formation.

text: A researcher studies an enzyme before and after exposure to high temperature. The amino acid sequence is unchanged, but the enzyme no longer binds its substrate well. Which conclusion is most reasonable?
choices: Heat likely disrupted higher-level structure needed to maintain the active site
The enzyme lost all carbon atoms
The enzyme was converted into DNA
The enzyme gained extra amino acids during heating
explain: Denaturation alters secondary/tertiary structure and can destroy the active site without changing primary sequence.
